title: Director, Medical Affairs
description:
PTC Therapeutics is a global commercial biopharmaceutical company. For over 25 years our team has been deeply committed to a unified purpose: Extending life’s moments for children and adults living with a rare disease.


At PTC, we cultivate an inclusive culture where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered. We welcome candidates from all backgrounds to join our team**,** fostering a strong sense of belonging.


Visit our website to learn more about our company and culture!  

Site: www.ptcbio.com

**Job Description Summary:**
----------------------------

The Director, Medical Affairs is responsible for providing strategic and operational leadership to the Medical Affairs function for the respective country or region of responsibility to support marketed and pipeline products. The incumbent is responsible for adapting global and regional medical affairs strategies to ensure that it is fit for purpose at the local/regional level.  

  

The Director, Medical Affairs supports and represents PTC and its products with, and to, key opinion leaders (KOL's), clinicians, academic institutions, and professional organizations within the assigned country or region. He/she addresses the specific needs of these clients by responding to unsolicited requests for information about PTC marketed and developing products.**Job Description:**
--------------------

**Responsibilities of the role include:**

* Adapts and influences the global, regional and country medical affairs strategy required in the country or region of responsibility.
* Manages, coaches and mentors a direct report
* Responsible for interactions with local authorities, local key opinion leaders (KOLs) and payers, patient organizations as well as other external stakeholders.
* Ensures execution of medical affairs strategy and builds and maintains trusted medical/scientific relationships with health community stakeholders.
* Collaborates with Marketing to ensure that the local strategic medical affairs plans are in line with the marketing plans/brand strategies.
* Drives the tactical implementation of medical affairs plans across the country, including but not limited to: KOL development and relationship building as well as the building of the scientific communications platform (publications, congresses etc.) and other related projects.
* Reviews promotional and non\-promotional materials with particular regard to medical accuracy and compliance to local regulation and code of practice.
* Manages and directs medical information process and service in close collaboration with other functions in medical affairs.
* Identifies local thought leaders, engages in scientific exchange and develops rapport with experts in the therapeutic area.
* Identifies, organizes and carry out regional medical initiatives (i.e. leads medical discussions at national advisory board meetings).
* Provides training and scientific education to commercial, medical teams and other internal stakeholders
* Provides support in the administration of clinical trials on request by Clinical Development teams; collaborates and provides support to other departments, especially to Regulatory Affairs and Pharmacovigilance (PV); represents Global Medical Affairs at local, regional and international scientific congresses.
* Perform other tasks and assignments as needed and specified by management.

**Requirements for the role include:**

* An advanced degree (e.g., Masters, PharmD, M.D. or Ph.D.) in a scientific discipline and demonstrated experience of progressive responsibility in, pharmaceutical, biotechnology or related environment with a minimum of 6 years medical affairs experience or equivalent combination of education and experience.
* People management experience is a must.
* Demonstrates leadership and the ability to manage and coach direct reports
* Hands\-on experience supporting the registration and launch of an orphan, rare disease, and/or specialized drug(s) in the country/region of responsibility.
* In\-depth, hands on experience working with KOL's, clinicians, HCPs, payors, and/or academic institutions establishing/maintaining relationships and scientific/medical credibility.
* Demonstrated success in interpreting scientific data and presenting research information to scientists and HCPs.
* Experience dealing with the local regulatory authorities and payor organizations.
* In\-depth experience adapting regional plans to a country orientation, while working within a global framework.
* Excellent working knowledge in local regulations and codes of practice as well as a good understanding of the market and medical dynamics in the designated territory.
* Ability to influence without direct authority.
* Proficiency with Microsoft Office.
* Excellent verbal and written communication and skills, including scientific/technical writing and presentations with the ability to communicate complex technical information clearly.
* Ability to work independently and collaboratively, as required, in a fast\-paced, matrixed, team environment consisting of internal and external team members.
* Analytical thinker with excellent problem\-solving skills and the ability to adapt to changing priorities and deadlines.
* Excellent planning, organization and time management skills including the ability to support and prioritize multiple projects.
* Demonstrates leadership and the ability to manage and coach direct reports.
* Fluent in Italian and English (written and verbal).

* Travel requirements.


Flexibility to travel domestically and internationally as needed by the business

**EEO Statement:**

**PTC Therapeutics is an equal opportunity employer. We welcome applications from all individuals, regardless of race, color, national origin, gender, age, physical characteristics, social origin, disability, religion, family status, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, disability, veteran status or any unlawful criterion under applicable law. We are committed to treating all applicants fairly and avoiding discrimination.**


Click here to return to the careers page
company_description: At PTC, it’s not simply a job to us. It’s a calling.

title: Manager, Marketing Production - Italy & Greece
description:
Netflix is one of the world's leading entertainment services, with over 300 million paid memberships in over 190 countries enjoying TV series, films and games across a wide variety of genres and languages. Members can play, pause and resume watching as much as they want, anytime, anywhere, and can change their plans at any time.
 

We are looking for a Marketing Production Manager to join our Italian Marketing team.


This is a full time contract position located in Rome.


The role will oversee campaign production \& project management across our marketing campaigns for films, series, and overarching initiatives. The role will require strong project management skills and production expertise, with an ability to juggle multiple projects and to navigate multiple tools \& complex workflows whilst maintaining creative excellence and operational best practices for the business.


This person will join our Marketing team and will work closely with cross\-functional partners across Netflix's Marketing and communications groups to connect our stories with millions of people worldwide.  

  



This person will manage the production and delivery of all marketing campaign assets for local and international titles across our Series, Films and Unscripted slates in Italy, ensuring that all the elements that go into making a great campaign are delivered on time and on budget. We are looking for a nimble, solutions\-oriented, project management expert, who thrives in a fast\-paced environment.


You will work closely with in\-house producers in our AV, Art \& Print, Digital as well as external creative and production agencies, to manage Netflix’s production and operational campaign needs. You will partner closely with marketing managers working across the Italian team, to ensure creative and operational excellence and solutions led project management approach to all the campaigns you are assigned to.  

  



You will serve as the connection point to cross\-functional stakeholders, facilitating alignment between global and local teams dedicated to creative, localisation, strategy, media, marketing, events, publicity and social.


The ideal candidate will be a creative production manager with a strong background in Media Production and a solid production local expertise with executional excellence and a drive for innovation and outstanding creativity.


**Key Responsibilities**

* Effectively managing the production of multiple campaign launches to ensure timely and accurate development, production and delivery of creative assets and consumer activations in the market both for Local and International titles and initiatives
* Being a thought partner with marketing managers on the creative development of local campaigns. Partner with them to provide production guidance, best practice advice, and innovative solutions for the creation and execution of campaign assets, stunts, and activations linked to the promotion of our titles
* End\-to\-end production management for marketing shoots (esp. AV), activations and asset creation for bespoke title campaigns
* Managing agency and vendor relationships by leading day\-to\-day production communications, project scope of work, production budgets and timelines
* Define, negotiate, communicate, implement and monitor technical quality standards and brand \& creative integrity on all project deliverables according to Netflix guidelines
* Driving and enabling production excellence and innovation across channels like Social, Digital, OOH and Experiential
* Project managing the delivery of integrated marketing campaigns, including online and offline video, out of home, print, digital display, TV, cinema, radio and paid social
* Support our Italian marketing team by planning overall campaign production timelines, budget management, project deliverables and campaign resources. Partner with Events, Photo and AV Studio to deliver best in class events, activations and assets creation. Ensure a regular flow of information between all cross\-functional partners in Italy, EMEA and Globally. Regularly share updates on project timelines and budgets and escalate any anticipated production issues
* Operational Strategist \& Project Management. Proactively identify business\-critical issues and opportunities to improve how we track, produce, share, utilize, and traffic promotional assets at Netflix, balancing the short and long\-term needs of multiple cross\-functional Marketing and Communications teams. Elevate our project management functions to enable flexible processes and structure, while also driving accountability for process and workflows improvement
* Nurture and maintain a network of best\-in market vendors (production companies, freelancers)

**Experience \& Skills**

* 8 or more years of marketing creative production \& project management crafting world class campaigns and executions. Experience within the entertainment industry and International experience are a plus
* Knowledgeable of the evolving Italian and Global advertising and media landscape, including exposure to/experience with digital marketing and media
* Passion for storytelling and high competency and proven track record of project management for high profile local and global campaigns
* Expert knowledge of marketing campaign and localisation workflows, including understanding the creative and production process for AV, Print \& Digital deliverables
* Fluent in AV, Print \& Digital types and formats, whilst understanding the fundamentals of asset management, servicing and ad trafficking
* Great agility to oversee a wide range of production types (shoots, on set and digital activations, media assets, stunts) and a high volume of creative and campaign types in parallel
* Exceptionally strong communication and analytical skills along with the ability to think strategically and creatively
* Proactive, fast, and flexible problem\-solver with superb time, resource, and stakeholder management
* A strong appetite for innovation, an open and solution\-oriented mindset
* An entertainment fan who understands the power of entertainment to transform behaviors and influence pop culture
* Ability to thrive in a fast\-paced and demanding environment, possessing a high level of intellectual curiosity, and demonstrate strong and effective judgment in the face of ambiguity
* Fluency in Italian and English is required; please submit your CV in English

  

*At Netflix, we’re committed to paying our employees equitably and increasing our pay transparency. Our compensation structure consists solely of an annual salary; we do not have bonuses. To determine your personal top of market compensation, we rely on market indicators and consider objective and gender\-neutral criteria (e.g. your specific job family, background, skills, and experience). The range for this role is typically €85,000 \- €135,000\.*

Inclusion is a Netflix value and we strive to host a meaningful interview experience for all candidates. If you want an accommodation/adjustment for a disability or any other reason during the hiring process, please send a request to your recruiting partner.


We are an equal\-opportunity employer and celebrate diversity, recognizing that diversity builds stronger teams. We approach diversity and inclusion seriously and thoughtfully. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, ancestry, national origin, caste, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity or expression, age, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, genetic makeup, marital status, or military service.

title: Mechanical Engineer II
description:
**Position Summary**
--------------------



We are looking for a candidate that will join our team as a Mechanical Engineer II .


As a Mechanical Engineer II within the Sustaining Engineering team of the GI Reprocessing R\&D department, this role supports the ongoing function and performance testing of electromechanical capital equipment manufactured in Pomezia. The position focuses on planning, executing, and documenting structured verification and validation testing, as well as reproducing product issues and supporting root cause investigations.


Working closely with development engineers, laboratory technicians, quality engineers, and software validators, the role ensures testing is carried out in a consistent, data\-driven, and repeatable manner. The engineer contributes to identifying design improvements, strengthening product robustness, and supporting engineering changes to maintain high product quality in production and field use.

**Duties**
----------


* Performs structured testing of systems and subsystems using engineering principles to verify compliance with defined requirements.
* Conducts laboratory\-based troubleshooting by operating electromechanical equipment to reproduce failures, identify fault conditions, and understand system behavior under various use cases.
* Plans, coordinates, and executes feasibility studies, verification, and validation testing, and prepares technical documentation in compliance with applicable medical device standards.
* Performs root cause analysis and failure mode analysis to support the identification of effective corrective actions and design improvements.
* Designs and improves test methods, setups, and procedures to ensure testing is repeatable, reliable, and efficient.
* Collaborates cross\-functionally with engineering disciplines (software, control systems, chemical, microbiological), as well as quality and reliability teams, to ensure robust and well\-integrated product design
* Provides technical guidance and support on testing activities to engineers and laboratory technicians, contributing to knowledge sharing and consistent lab practices.
**Education Degree**
--------------------


* Bachelor's Degree in Mechanical Engineering
**Required Experience**
-----------------------


* Experience in defining, executing, and documenting structured test activities for electromechanical systems, including verification and validation testing (3 years).
* Practical experience with laboratory equipment such as measuring instruments, sensors, data acquisition systems, and oscilloscopes (3 years).
* Ability to analyze test data, interpret system behavior, and support troubleshooting and root cause investigations (1 year).
* Understanding of electromechanical systems, including mechanical, electrical, and fluid interactions in complex equipment (1 year).
* Experience in developing and standardizing test methods, test setups, and procedures to ensure repeatable and reliable results (1 year).
* Ability to use PLM Systems especially Windchill (1year).
* Experience in root cause analysis, risk assessment, and Failure Modes and Effects Analysis (FMEA) (1 year).
* Minimum working experiance required 3 years.
**Preferred Experience**
------------------------

  

* Engineering in regulated environment
* Theoretical and practical work in Laboratory
* Strong communication and problem\-solving skills
* Passion in searching and identifying technical risks and problems
* Organizational, communication, problem solving, and troubleshooting skills.
* English skills minimum on level B2\.
**Compensation**
----------------



In line with our commitment to transparency and equal pay, we provide the following information:* **Salary Range:** € 36\.00 \- 42\.000
* **NCBA:** Chimica , Concia e settori accorpati, CONFAPI.
* **Bonus:** This position is eligible for corporate bonus.

STERIS strives to be an Equal Opportunity Employer.

title: Field Clinical Specialist, Cardiac Rhythm Management (based in Rome)
description:
**Location:** based in the area of Rome, on a remote working schedule.

 **About the Role**  

The CRM (Cardiac Rhythm Management) Field Clinical Specialist will provide expert clinical product and technical assistance and training to physicians, CRM Lab Staff, and sales representatives on the effective and safe use of the CRM systems during case procedures within an assigned geography, and in a manner that leads to meeting and exceeding business goals.

 **In this role, you will be responsible to:**

* Serve as primary resource for clinical support in the areas of case coverage, basic troubleshooting, system/software development.
* Educate customers and internal Boston Scientific employees on the merits and proper clinical usage of CRM devices by giving presentations and demonstrations using a wide variety of formats and platforms (e.g., slides, transparencies, manuals) to keep all abreast of the latest product and technology developments.
* Attend cases in labs of hospital accounts for case support and development efforts of CRM system and its components.
* Provide initial and continuous, as needed, training and feedback for development team and sales representatives (as appropriate) on CRM system, development process, products and features, and procedures involving those products and features.
* Meet with existing and potential clients (e.g., physicians, physician office groups at hospitals) by traveling (in an automobile or airplane) to identify their clinical needs, goals, and constraints related to patient care and to discuss and demonstrate how company products can help them to achieve their goals.
* Develop relationships with hospital personnel (e.g. through casual conversation, meetings, participation in conferences) to make new contacts in other departments within hospital and to identify key decision makers in order to facilitate future sales.
* Respond to customer needs and complaints regarding products and service by developing creative and feasible solutions or working with other related personnel (e.g., sales, clinical research, marketing, technical support) to develop optimal solutions.

  



**What are we looking for in you:**

* Young professionals with minimum experience (at least 1 year preferably) in a similar role, preferably within the cardiology area.
* Experience in international markets would be a plus.
* Exposure to the Cardiac Rhythm Management market would be a plus.
* Strong communication skills.
* Ability to understand and communicate complex technical and clinical details.
* Fluent in English (B2 or higher). Also, additional languages skills are appreciated.
* Able to rapidly adapt to a very dynamic marketplace.
* Strong team player, collaborative, ability to build relationships and work cross\-functionally.
* Flexible, adaptable but focused and persistent.
* Willing and able to travel extensively as required by workload
* Based in the area of Rome.

 **What We Can Offer to You:**

* **Contract Type:** Permanent Contract.
* **Expected Salary:** € 28\.592 \- € 37\.527 (base salary).
* **Applicable NCBA/CCNL:** Commercio, Terziario, Distribuzione e Servizi.

  

In addition to your salary, Boston Scientific offers the following benefits:

  

* Variable Part of Salary: Sales Incentive Plan, based on individual sales performance.
* Health Insurance (based on NCBA) \& Supplementary Health Insurance (covering for life and accidents), different based on NCBA level.
* EMEA Paid Parental Leave Policy.
* Paid Leave for Medical Appointments.
* In\-Office Gym.
* Virtual Learning Tools.
* Educational Assistance Program.
* Employee Assistance Program (EAP).
* Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP): ability to invest 1–10% of annual salary.
* Business Travel Insurance on medical incidents, security incidents and loss or theft of personal ID documents.
* In\-House Tax Assistance Service for 730 Tax Return.
* Company Car.
* Healthy work\-life balance with flexibility and respect for personal time.
* Working in an international environment with diverse, high\-performing colleagues.
* Inspirational colleagues \& culture that empower creativity, accountability, and excellence.
* Career Development opportunities through dedicated training programs and stretch assignments.

 ***We, Boston Scientific, are an equal employment opportunity employer. We do not and will not make any personnel decisions (like recruiting, hiring, job assignments, and promotions) based on a person’s age, color, national origin, citizenship status, physical or mental disability, race, religion, creed, gender, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, genetic information, marital status, status with regard to public assistance, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local law.***
company_description: Boston Scientific is dedicated to transforming lives through innovative medical solutions that improve the health of patients around the world.

title: Investigation & Client Quality Specialist
description:
**Work Schedule**

**Environmental Conditions**

**Job Description**


As part of the Thermo Fisher Scientific team, you’ll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Join our colleagues in bringing our Mission to life every single day to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. We provide our global teams with the resources needed to achieve individual career goals while helping to take science a step beyond by developing solutions for some of the world’s toughest challenges, like protecting the environment, making sure our food is safe or helping find cures for cancer.  

  

DESCRIPTION:  

At Thermo Fisher Scientific, you'll join a quality assurance team focused on ensuring consistent execution of quality standards and regulatory compliance. You'll play a vital role in enabling our customers to make breakthrough discoveries and innovations that make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. You'll ensure product quality, drive continuous improvement initiatives, and collaborate with cross\-functional teams to maintain the highest standards of quality and compliance in a GMP environment.  

  

REQUIREMENTS:  

* Bachelor's Degree required, no prior experience required. Experience in pharmaceutical, medical device or regulated industry preferred
* Preferred Fields of Study: Chemistry, Biology, Biotechnology, Engineering, or related scientific/technical field
* Additional certifications in Quality Systems or GMP preferred
* Strong knowledge of cGMP, ISO standards (13485/9001\), and international regulatory requirements
* Experience with quality systems and documentation including batch records, SOPs, deviations, CAPA
* Proficiency in quality management software systems (TrackWise, SAP, etc.)
* Demonstrated ability to perform detailed documentation review and quality assessments
* Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills for quality investigations and root cause analysis
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills
* Ability to work independently and collaborate effectively with cross\-functional teams
* Experience conducting internal quality audits and supporting external regulatory audits
* Proficient computer skills including Microsoft Office applications
* Strong attention to detail and commitment to accuracy
* Ability to prioritize and adapt in a changing environment
* Experience with continuous improvement methodologies (Lean, Six Sigma) preferred
* May require up to 10% travel
* Must be able to work in controlled environments wearing required PPE
company_description: At Thermo Fisher Scientific, our professionals develop critical solutions—and build rewarding careers.

title: Wind Farm Supervisor
description:
**Standort(e):**  

Rome, 62, IT, 00192
  
  

RWE Renewables Italia S.r.l.
  
To start as soon as possible, full time, permanent
  
  

**Functional area:** Operations \& Maintenance
  
**Remuneration:** EUR 41,500 – EUR 69,500 (Annual gross Total Target Compensation)
  
  

The job reports to the Regional Site Manager.
  
  

Key Responsibilities
  
  

Manage the operation and maintenance of wind farms efficiently and effectively, in accordance with best practices and technical excellence objectives, through internal resources and/or third\-party suppliers.
  
Manage and oversee third\-party suppliers within the assigned geographical area from both a technical and HSE (Health, Safety and Environment) perspective, as well as from an economic standpoint.
  
Manage balance of plant (BOP) operation and maintenance activities, including civil works, substations and associated infrastructure.
  
Maintain relationships with the Grid Manager, ENEL and Terna to support the safe and efficient operation of local wind farms.
  
Collect and analyse operational data, preparing reports on production, availability, maintenance, spare parts management and related activities.
  
Prepare monthly management reports, including budget versus actual comparisons for both technical and financial aspects.
  
Support the Regional Site Manager in managing relationships with RWE business functions, monitor supplier performance from a contractual perspective, and provide feedback on service quality to support supplier selection and evaluation.
  
Apply O\&M and HSSE business processes, proposing improvements to enhance operational effectiveness and efficiency.
  
  

Interfaces
  
  

Regional Site Manager
  
Main Components Manager
  
BOP Asset Leaders
  
Head of O\&M
  
Operations Specialist
  
  

Key Skills Required
  
  

Diploma from an Industrial Technical Institute in Mechanical, Electrical or Electronic Engineering, with 2–5 years of experience in maintenance management and/or the operation of renewable energy plants and asset management.
  
Previous experience in O\&M activities for renewable energy generation facilities.
  
Ability to work across both mechanical and electrical/electronic disciplines, with appropriate training where required.
  
Knowledge of key environmental regulations and health and safety standards applicable to industrial plants.
  
Experience managing relationships with organisations and/or companies responsible for the operation and/or maintenance of production plants.
  
Ability to implement operational procedures and maintenance management processes.
  
Previous experience in medium\-sized or large organisations is preferred.
  
Good written and spoken English, with native\-level fluency in Italian.
  
Excellent knowledge of Microsoft Office applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, etc.).
  
Knowledge of CAD.
  
Knowledge of SAP would be an advantage.
  
  

Your Benefits
  
  

We aim to make your time with us as rewarding as possible by fostering a supportive and inclusive environment. Alongside competitive pay and opportunities for growth, we care about your well\-being and the balance between work and life. Our benefits include:
  
  

Flexible and hybrid working, enabling a balance between remote work and in\-office collaboration
  
Company pension scheme to help secure your future
  
Employee stock programme giving you a stake in our success
  
Training and development opportunities to support your professional growth
  
  

**Apply with just a few clicks:** ad code 92775, application period: 15/07/2026
  
**Any questions? Contact HR:** Ivan Malcolm D'Silva, ivan.dsilva@rwe.com
  
  

We look forward to meeting you. Of course, you can find us on LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, YouTube and Xing, too.
  
  

We value diversity and therefore welcome all applications \- regardless of gender, disability, nationality, ethnic and social origin, religion/belief, age, sexual orientation, and identity. \#inclusionmatters
  
  

RWE Renewables Europe \& Australia is a leading player in the renewable energy sector with over 30 years of experience. We currently operate onshore wind farms, solar plants and battery storage systems with around 5 gigawatts of installed capacity in our core markets in Europe and Australia.
  
  

Supported by a workforce of around 2,000 employees, we continue to expand our renewable generation capacity in a value\-accretive way. Our expertise spans the entire project lifecycle: we develop, plan, finance, build and operate our projects. With a strong project pipeline, we are working with communities to create a long\-term value for everyone, today and for the future.
company_description: RWE is leading the way to a green energy world. With an extensive investment and growth strategy. Around 19,000 employees worldwide work towards our purpose: Our energy for a sustainable life.
